name: Chocolate Swirl Babka
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the first two ingredients until smooth.
2. Add sugar and blend.
3. Add flour and mix until crumbly.
4. Sprinkle yeast over warm water. Add 1 tsp sugar. Let stand for 5 minutes.
5. Add sugar, milk, salt, vanilla, and butter. Mix in 2-1/2 cups of flour to blend.
6. Beat on medium until smooth and elastic (about 5 minutes).
7. Separate 1 egg; save the white for glaze. Beat yolk and whole eggs one at a time into the mixture. Add 1-1/2 cups more flour.
8. Turn dough onto lightly floured board or cloth. Knead 10-15 minutes until small bubbles appear in the dough. Continue adding small amounts of flour as needed.
9. Turn dough into greased bowl and cover. Let rise for doubled, about 30-45 minutes.
10. Punch down dough and invert bowl over dough. Let rest for 10 minutes.
11. Divide dough in half. Roll out into 2 10x20-inch rectangles.
12. Brush each rectangle with 1/2 of melted butter, leaving a 1/2 inch margin around all edges.
13. Blend cocoa and sugar until there are no lumps. Sprinkle 1/2 of the cocoa mixture over each rectangle, then sprinkle 1/2 of nuts over each.
14. Beginning with long sides, roll jellyroll fashion and pinch to seal. Place rectangles zig-zag in a 4-1/2 x 8-1/2-inch loaf pans.
15. Let rise for doubled about 45 minutes.
16. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
17. Beat egg white with 1 tsp water. Brush dough with glaze.
18. Mix topping ingredients to form coarse crumbs and sprinkle over.
19. Bake for 30-35 minutes. Carefully remove from pans and let cool on a rack.
